criteria_checked.note: Goal, D1-D4 decomposition, and out-of-scope list are unambiguous. r1 clarity nit RESOLVED: the DEFAULT in-tree ledger .harness/metrics/ledger.jsonl is now explicitly special-cased ALLOW (lines 17-22, 128-134), with only the --ledger override routed through the out_refused-style fail-closed check — verified correct against out_refused (dmc-v0.5.0-run-metrics.sh:33 refuses ALL in-tree paths).

REQUIRED r1 fix #2 (AC5 vacuous-pass) genuinely closed: the default-path AC (lines 193-198) now requires exit 0 AND test -f .harness/metrics/ledger.jsonl with >=1 line AND git status clean, ALL together, and states 'a silently-refused default write must FAIL this AC'. A broken/refused default write can no longer pass for the wrong reason. AC set remains strong (AC1 append-only + byte-identical-on-refuse, AC2 leak grep -c 0, AC3 rollup determinism + skipped_malformed, AC4 CLI-vs-direct byte-compare, new AC offline self-audit).

All repo findings re-verified accurate this pass: frozen --validate exits 1 on invalid (line 84); deterministic emit lines 88-102 with run_id/goal_type/efficiency_notes each redacted on their own line + newline-collapse (line 54); scope-guard exempts .harness/evidence + .harness/verification but NOT .harness/metrics (scope-guard.sh:160-166); .gitignore lacks .harness/metrics/ (D4 adds it); .harness/mode=active; branch claude/dmc-fable-core at 3e0caf1. Both frozen selectors currently self-test green (effort 14/0, course 20/0).

REQUIRED r1 fix #1 (field-count off-by-one) genuinely resolved: I re-counted the frozen REQ array (dmc-v0.5.0-run-metrics.sh:55-57) = 20 keys, and the schema JSON block (run-metrics.schema.md:9-30) = 20; Rev 2 Finding (line 61) says '20 required fields', AC1 (lines 168-170) is count-agnostic ('the frozen validator's full REQ set — 20 keys; count-checked against the REQ array, not a literal'). All path/line refs correct.

Redaction-parity design remains SOUND: free-form values (run_id/goal_type/efficiency_notes) are taken from the frozen REDACTED emit, numerics/enums from the validated input — the only secret-carriers pass the frozen redactor, numerics fail-close a secret-shaped string, enums are fixed sets. AC2 leak-probe genuinely falsifies a 'read raw input' implementation. Append-only + no-gate-reads-ledger + no-auto-invocation stay internally consistent.

5 in-scope files with correct grants (new .py=create, 4 additive edits). Legacy 802/3/3 invariance CONFIRMED: PINNED_BASELINE={49,802,3,3} (dmc-legacy-selftest.py:118) + hardcoded NOFLAG_TOOLS/SELFTEST_TOOLS; mirror stray-guard greps f.startswith('dmc-v0.') (line 208) so the deliberate name dmc-metrics-recorder.py (no v0. prefix) avoids both the legacy count and the stray-copy FAIL — a hard build constraint already in the plan. DEFAULT_PROTECTED (gate-check-runner:22-31) excludes bin/dmc + bin/lib, so no DMC_GATE_PROTECTED override needed (plan correct); enforcement-class FLAG expected non-degrading per v1.0.5 precedent. Revision notes (lines 264-272) match the actual Rev 2 text; no undisclosed scope change (file count still 5, out-of-scope list intact).

Security lens (mandatory — enforcement-class landmark bin/dmc + new bin/lib). RECOMMENDED r1 item FOLDED: AC4 (lines 183-188) now adds a structural self-audit mirroring the frozen AC6 (dmc-v0.5.0-run-metrics.sh:169-174) — greps the recorder's operative source for os.environ/getenv/socket/urllib/requests/curl/wget/--live => 0 matches, only subprocess target the pinned validator, wired as a self-test assertion. Primary leak vector (secret in free-form field) closed by frozen-redactor parity + AC2. Post-suspend ordering rationale FOLDED (Risks line 210): correctly characterized as snapshot-integrity protection, not denial-avoidance (bash-radius does not fire on the write-idiom-free `dmc metrics record` and scope-guard adjudicates only Edit/Write). ADVISORY (non-blocking): to faithfully mirror AC6 the self-audit needs the AUDIT_BLOCK sentinel so the grep pattern-literals in the Python self-test are not self-matched — 'comments/docstrings excluded' under-specifies this, but a miss produces a loud false-failure, not a vacuous pass.

Fully additive: new .py is deletable; the 4 edits are append-only (.gitignore line, two doc sections, one MILESTONES entry). Legacy count + mirror-check invariant under the chosen name, so revert is clean. No migration, no state, no frozen-tool mutation. ADVISORY (non-blocking): the default-path ALLOW branch is exercised only by the single post-suspend probe (self-tests use an out-of-tree --ledger), but that probe is itself the anti-vacuous AC (exit 0 + test -f + >=1 line), so the branch is covered.
